experiment: DOMEX-CAT-S435 — FMEA refresh + NAT check + MINIMAL->ADEQUATE analysis
method: FMEA audit — review all 28 FMs from S420 registry against S429-S435 commits (30 commits across 7 sessions). Verified maintenance modularization (9 modules extracted from maintenance.py S422/S433). Checked FM-19/20/22/24 defense layers via code review of check.sh, orient.py, open_lane.py. Read 7 hardening artifacts (S422-S432). Analyzed filtering domain work (L-1005/L-1007/L-1008) for new FM candidates. Verified S427 mass-deletion incident (3033 files, commit 497a94ef).
expect: FMEA refresh finds >=1 new FM from S429-S435 changes; MINIMAL->ADEQUATE path identified for severity-1 FMs
actual: 2 new FMs found (FM-29 module-extraction import chain fragility, FM-30 cross-layer filter cascade blind spot). Total 28->30 FMs. 4 status upgrades since S420 registry: FM-19 UNMITIGATED->PARTIAL, FM-20 UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL, FM-22 UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL, FM-24 UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL. Current distribution: 0 INADEQUATE, 11 MINIMAL, 7 PARTIAL, 6 ADEQUATE, 6 UNMITIGATED. NAT prediction from S420 (~S435) CONFIRMED (S427 mass-deletion incident + S435 new FMs, 0-8 session error). MINIMAL->ADEQUATE upgrade paths identified for all 4 severity-1 MINIMAL FMs; 3 of 4 are low-effort.
diff: Expected >=1 new FM; found 2 — stronger than expected. FM-29 (modular extraction fragility) is a direct consequence of S422/S433 maintenance.py extraction work — the hardening activity itself introduced a new correlated-failure vector (defense-in-depth creates new coupling). FM-30 (cross-layer filter cascade blind spot) emerged from filtering domain work (L-1005/L-1007/L-1008) — not a new failure but a newly identified blind spot in the existing architecture. S420 NAT predicted external-interface class; actual is modular-architecture class. Migration pattern extended: infrastructure -> system-design -> concurrency -> epistemology -> modular-architecture. 3 of 4 severity-1 MINIMAL upgrade paths are low-effort, which is stronger than expected — actionable in 1-2 sessions.

FM-19.evidence: S422: stale_write_check.py in check.sh (1 enforced automated layer, hard-blocks content-loss APPEND/MIXED commits). S432 replication: 50% raw collision reduction (173->87 events), 28% per-session-normalized (17.3->12.4). Defense confirmed operational — blocked 3 files (PHILOSOPHY.md, PRINCIPLES.md, FRONTIER.md) with content-loss risk. Still PARTIAL: collision surface SPREAD (22->35 files) even as event density decreased. Content-hash comparison before write not yet implemented (upgrade path for 3rd layer).

FM-20.evidence: S427: orient_checks.py check_stale_baselines() scanner wired into orient.py output. Detects 4 pattern types in tools/*.py (session_constant, fallback_comment, estimated_sessions, session_default). 2 critical time-bombs fixed: frontier_triage.py S393 fallback (27s stale), eval_sufficiency.py S193 fallback (234s stale). Mean staleness confirmed 173 sessions (worsened from 63 at S398). Detection is comprehensive but non-blocking (advisory only). 1 automated layer.

FM-22.evidence: S429: open_lane.py domain-frontier staleness gate added. BLOCK new DOMEX lanes for domains >50 sessions stale (47% of domains, 20/43). WARN >30 sessions stale (9%, 4/43). Escape valve: --force flag. 1 automated creation-time enforcement layer (L-601). Gate correctly fires: linguistics (240s stale) BLOCKED, catastrophic-risks (19s stale) passed.

FM-24.evidence: S428: check.sh FM-24 prescriptive-without-enforcement detector. Scans staged lessons for ## Rule/## Prescription without tool/file references. NOTICE-level only (does not block commits). 340/644 prescriptive lessons lack enforcement paths. L-988 correctly flagged as test case. 1 automated advisory layer (not enforced — most vulnerable FM to regression per L-601 theorem).

FM-01.evidence: S427 incident: 3033 files deleted by concurrent session commit 497a94ef, restored within same session by 2a743a8f. Guards (STAGED_DELETIONS threshold >20, mass-staging >100) fire per-session but cannot prevent a DIFFERENT concurrent session from staging and committing. FM-01 is ADEQUATE for self-inflicted deletions but the cross-session bypass path is FM-09 territory.

new_fms_found.name: Module extraction import chain fragility
new_fms_found.description: maintenance.py extracted to 9 modules across S422/S433 (maintenance_common, _health, _quality, _signals, _lanes, _domains, _state, _drift, _inventory). All modules import from maintenance_common.py. A single breakage in maintenance_common.py (rename, syntax error, missing constant) cascades to all 9 modules simultaneously, disabling the entire maintenance system. Unlike monolithic maintenance.py where a single function error was isolated by try/except, the shared-import architecture creates correlated failure at the module level. The extract-as-is pattern (L-941/L-1010) reduces risk during extraction but does not protect against post-extraction drift. Verified clean S435 (all 47 check functions delegate correctly, 0 import errors).
defenses: maintenance.py try/except around each check_fn() call (catches ImportError per-function, continues other checks — isolation preserved)
Rule: maintenance_common.py changes require testing all 9 modules
new_fms_found.upgrade_path: Add import-health smoke test: check.sh or orient.py verifies all 9 maintenance_*.py modules import successfully before running checks. Low effort.

new_fms_found.name: Cross-layer filter cascade blind spot
new_fms_found.description: L-1005 (S433) identifies 14 independent filtering mechanisms across 6 layers (context, knowledge, attention, quality, temporal, epistemic). No tool monitors cross-layer propagation. A temporal filter failure (stale baseline per FM-20) silently corrupts quality filter output (false URGENT) which misallocates attention. L-555 exemplar: one stale baseline caused an entire wasted session. L-1007 confirms 5 cascade events across 50 sessions (100% session rate, 62% with 3-layer cascades). L-1008 DISPUTES architectural causation (independence ratio ~1.0 for 5/6 layer pairs), finding cascades are bug-class. Regardless of causation, the blind spot exists: zero tools detect when a failure in one filter layer propagates downstream. Each filter is optimized independently (compact.py, UCB1, check.sh, orient.py baselines). Compound FNR = 1-Prod(1-FNR_i) can theoretically reach 26.5% if each of 6 layers has 5% FNR.
defenses: Rule: L-1007 prescribes cross-layer cascade monitor (not yet built)
new_fms_found.last_incident: endemic (L-555 exemplar, L-1007 identifies 5 cascade events in 50 sessions)
new_fms_found.upgrade_path: Build cascade_monitor.py that detects correlated failures across layers using maintenance_outcomes.json (already captures per-check results by session). Flag sessions where temporal+quality or quality+attention checks fail simultaneously. Wire as periodic (cadence ~10). Medium effort.

upgrade_paths.name: WSL filesystem corruption
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add check.sh pre-commit guard: stat CORE.md, SWARM.md, INDEX.md — if any returns inaccessible, FAIL. Adds 2nd automated enforced layer, crossing ADEQUATE threshold.

upgrade_paths.name: Genesis replay (stale genesis propagation)
upgrade_paths.current_layers: 1 automated enforced (check.sh genesis hash verification) + 2 rules
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add orient.py genesis hash check at session start (compare computed hash to workspace/genesis-bundle-*.hash). Currently only checked at commit time — a session with tampered genesis has zero detection until commit. genesis_hash.py already exists, just needs calling from orient.py.

upgrade_paths.name: PreCompact state loss
upgrade_paths.current_layers: 2 automated (pre-compact-checkpoint.py + check.sh) but recovery path undocumented
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Document recovery procedure. Inject checkpoint content as orient.py context preamble on compaction resume. Add automated test that pre-compact-checkpoint.py output is parseable by orient.py. The 2 existing layers need validation to count as independent.

upgrade_paths.name: Ghost lesson resurrection after compaction
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add compact.py post-compaction verification step: after archiving, scan git status for unexpected 'new file' additions in memory/lessons/ and auto-unstage them. Alternatively, add orient.py working-tree ghost scan at session start. Either adds a 2nd automated layer.

upgrade_paths.name: Git index corruption
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add orient.py git index health check at session start: verify git status succeeds and index file size is above minimum threshold. Auto-recover with rm .git/index && git read-tree HEAD if corrupted.

upgrade_paths.name: Belief cascade propagation (colony drift)
upgrade_paths.current_layers: 1 automated enforced (check.sh colony drift guard) + 1 rule
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add belief velocity monitor: track CORE.md/PHILOSOPHY.md change frequency per session. Flag if >2 belief-layer changes in a single session (high cascade risk). Wire into maintenance.py.

upgrade_paths.name: sync_state.py corrupt count injection
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Already at 2 automated layers. Borderline ADEQUATE. Add unit test verifying zero-count guard fires correctly under concurrent load.

upgrade_paths.name: Belief injection via bulletin board
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add bulletin content validation in bulletin.py: reject bulletins targeting beliefs/CORE.md unless T1 authority verified. NEVER-REMOVE guard catches deletions but not subtle modifications.

upgrade_paths.name: Fork bomb (uncontrolled colony spawning)
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add orient.py colony count check: count active colony processes/directories at session start. Flag if >5 active colonies. Adds independent 2nd automated layer.

upgrade_paths.name: Concurrent lesson number collision
upgrade_paths.current_layers: 1 automated enforced (lesson_collision_check.py in check.sh) + 1 advisory (claim.py) + 1 rule
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Enforce slot reservation in lesson-creation flow: lesson template tool should call claim.py automatically. Currently advisory — sessions can skip it. Making claim.py required adds 2nd enforced layer.

upgrade_paths.name: Observer baseline staleness cascade
upgrade_paths.upgrade_action: Add maintenance.py check_observer_staleness() as DUE-level check (not just orient.py advisory). Auto-refresh dispatch_calibration.json via periodic. Adds 2nd automated layer.

severity_1_upgrade_summary.lowest_effort_critical_upgrade: FM-02 (WSL filesystem corruption): add check.sh stat guard on core files. Low effort, adds 2nd automated layer -> ADEQUATE.
recommended_priority_order: FM-02 (low effort, CRITICAL — stat check in check.sh)
FM-11 (low effort, CRITICAL — genesis hash check in orient.py)
FM-06 (low effort, CRITICAL — document recovery + orient.py checkpoint inject)
FM-03 (medium effort, CRITICAL — compact.py post-archive verification)

nat_analysis.actual_incident: S427 mass-deletion (3033 files, FM-01/FM-09 class) + S435 new FMs (FM-29, FM-30)
nat_analysis.interval_analysis: S302->S351(49s), S351->S403(52s), S403->S410(7s), S410->S420(10s), S420->S427(7s)/S435(15s). Post-acceleration: stabilizing at 7-15 session intervals.
nat_analysis.fm_class_prediction: S420 predicted external-interface failures (F-COMP1). INCORRECT — F-COMP1 inquiry did not produce a failure.
nat_analysis.fm_class_actual: System-design (FM-29 module extraction) + epistemological (FM-30 cross-layer cascade). Migration pattern extended: infrastructure -> system-design -> concurrency -> epistemology -> modular-architecture.
nat_analysis.next_prediction: ~S450-S465. Most probable: dependency chain failures as module count grows (9 maintenance modules + 3+ orient modules + 120+ tools). The import-chain coupling from FM-29 will be tested when maintenance_common.py constants are next updated.

s427_mass_deletion.root_cause: Concurrent session ran git add -A or equivalent. Per-session check.sh guards cannot prevent cross-session contamination.
s427_mass_deletion.defense_gap: FM-01 ADEQUATE for self-inflicted; FM-09 ADEQUATE for detection. But restoration required manual intervention. No automated rollback mechanism.
s427_mass_deletion.lesson: Defense-in-depth at the per-session level does not prevent repo-level contamination in concurrent environments.

s433_maintenance_extraction.description: maintenance.py 13266t -> 5149t (61% reduction). 3 new modules (maintenance_health.py, maintenance_quality.py, maintenance_signals.py). All 47 check functions now delegate to 9 modules.
s433_maintenance_extraction.fm_impact: Introduces FM-29 (import chain fragility). Mitigated by try/except isolation in maintenance.py main loop.

s434_filtering_domain.description: L-1005/L-1007/L-1008 filtering domain work. L-1005 identifies 16.1% invisible knowledge. L-1007 confirms 5 cascade events in 50 sessions. L-1008 disputes architectural causation (independence ratio ~1.0).
s434_filtering_domain.fm_impact: Reveals FM-30 (cross-layer filter cascade blind spot). Zero tools monitor cross-layer propagation.
